content: CORRECTED diagnosis of dead bench SO-101 (on /dev/ttyACM1; Reachy Mini holds /dev/ttyACM0). Symptom: all motors collide at factory id 1 (read id1 -> COMM_RX_CORRUPT, ids 2-6 -> TIMEOUT). EARLIER LOCK THEORY WAS WRONG: the STS3215 EEPROM Lock register (addr55=1) does NOT block the ID write on fw 3.10 — proven by writing id 1->2 while Lock=1 and it took. The low-level write works: official `arm101 set-motor-id 1 --apply` moved a single isolated motor 2->1 and it persisted across a fresh process. REAL ROOT CAUSE = the setup-motors/arm-setup WALK collapses chained motors: it auto-detects whatever id is on the bus and writes new_id to detected_id, walking gripper=6 down to shoulder_pan=1, with NO guard that only one motor is present and NO read-back verify. On an assembled (chained) arm where all motors share id 1, every step writes its id to ALL motors at once; they move in lockstep and the final step (shoulder_pan->1) leaves ALL motors at id 1. Tool reports success throughout. CODE GAPS to fix: (1) setup/set-motor-id must detect >1 responder on the bus and refuse with guidance; (2) must read the id back after write and confirm the unique id stuck. FIX to bring arm alive: physically isolate each motor (unplug daisy-chain) and assign + verify one at a time; the bus fundamentally requires isolation. -7=COMM_RX_CORRUPT (collision), -6=COMM_RX_TIMEOUT (absent).

VALIDATED on bench STS3215 (fw3.10) on /dev/ttyACM1: single-motor id assignment + gentle motion both work end-to-end. (1) Official `arm101 set-motor-id N --apply` correctly writes+persists id on a SINGLE isolated motor (Lock=1 does NOT block it). (2) Gentle move recipe (live-confirmed, operator saw it move ~26deg and back, smooth): STS3215 motion registers = addr41 Acceleration(1B), addr42 Goal_Position(2B), addr46 Goal/Running Speed(2B). Used acc=20, speed=500, +/-300 ticks; present_load stayed ~230-236 during FREE gentle motion (this is the no-contact baseline -> the --gentle contact threshold must sit ABOVE ~250 free-motion load). enable_torque(False)=limp at end. NOTE bus.py has write_goal_position + enable_torque but NO write_goal_speed/accel helper -> add them (or raw write2ByteTxRx addr46 / write1ByteTxRx addr41) when building arm flex --gentle. Procedure to bring up the assembled arm: each remaining motor ships at factory id 1, so it MUST be assigned ALONE (connect one, set id, verify, next) else it collides with the already-assigned id-1 motor. links: arm101-duplicate-id-diagnosis-2026-06-30

ROOT CAUSE + FIX of arm101 dead-arm: id/baud EEPROM writes never opened the STS3215 Lock register (addr55), so on fw3.10 the write took effect LIVE but did NOT commit to EEPROM and reverted to the factory value on the next power-cycle. That is why every assigned id silently fell back to id 1 after assembly/power-off, leaving all 6 motors colliding at id 1. PROVEN on hardware: unlock(addr55=0) -> write id -> relock(addr55=1) makes the id survive a real power-cycle (motor read back id 2 cold). FIX APPLIED in arm101/hardware/bus.py: FeetechBus._set_lock(motor,locked) helper added; write_id_baudrate now does _set_lock(motor,False) before the baud+id writes and _set_lock(new_id,True) after (relock addressed to the NEW id since the id write moves the device address); write_baudrate wraps its write the same way (relock at same id). Updated 2 tests in tests/test_bus.py that asserted the old no-lock sequence; full suite 385 passing. NOTE: lock does NOT block the live write (earlier wrong theory) - it blocks PERSISTENCE only. Still-open follow-ups: set-motor-id/setup-motors have no read-back verify and no multi-motor-on-bus guard (these would have surfaced the silent revert); fold into the doctor hardware-probe spec. supersedes earlier confusion in arm101-duplicate-id-diagnosis-2026-06-30.

Live gentle-flex touch-detection VALIDATED on assembled SO-101 follower gripper (id6). Incremental compliant close (25-tick steps, ACC=15 addr41, speed=300 addr46, slow): present_load ramped 140 (moving) -> 208 -> 320; threshold 250 triggered TOUCH at pos 2979/load 320, backed off 70 ticks and held, then enable_torque(False)=limp. Tuning insight for arm flex --gentle: gripper free-motion load is HIGHER than a big joint (~140-208 even moving slowly, due to gear friction) so the contact threshold must be PER-JOINT (gripper ~250-300; bigger joints lower). This confirms the spec parked-unknown: present_load contact threshold needs per-joint calibration + --threshold override. Whole-arm read also confirmed: all 6 ids 1-6 respond clean (shoulder_pan1 shoulder_lift2 elbow_flex3 wrist_flex4 wrist_roll5 gripper6) ~12V each after the eeprom-lock fix. links: arm101-eeprom-lock-persistence-fix-2026-06-30 arm101-gentle-move-validated-2026-06-30

gentle_move samples present_load BEFORE the servo physically moves, and tracks commanded ticks instead of measured position — proven on hardware 2026-07-12 during the first-ever `arm explore` hardware run (t12). EVIDENCE: a single gentle_move on wrist_roll (id 5, 400-tick travel, /dev/ttyACM1) RETURNED after 71ms claiming final_position=3548, but the joint was still at its start (3148) — it had not moved one tick. Real travel took ~900ms, entirely AFTER the call returned. Load during actual travel peaked at 272; every internal load read happened in the first 71ms while load was ~0-68. ROOT CAUSE (arm101/hardware/gentle.py stepping loop): `bus.write_goal_position(m, next)` then IMMEDIATELY `read_info(m)["present_load"]` with zero dwell (grep confirms NO sleep/wait/settle anywhere in arm101/hardware/), and `current = next_position` is the COMMANDED value so the loop exits when goal-writes are exhausted, not when the arm arrives; final_position=clamped_target is asserted, never measured. step=25 ticks => a 512-tick probe is ~20 writes in ~40ms, matching the observed ~60ms/probe. CONSEQUENCES: (1) contact detection is BLIND to contacts caused by the move itself — it only catches PRE-loaded joints (why the smoke run’s only contact was the gripper reading a stale 396, and likely why t7 rigid-stop passed: the arm was pre-pressed against the stop and the Torque_Limit cap of 500 was the real backstop, NOT the load-watch); (2) every `arm explore` reachable verdict is asserted from commanded ticks, never verified — the v0.16/v0.17 map is fiction (smoke run: load_magnitude exactly 0 on 9 of 10 probes); (3) the PR #31 per-joint DEFAULT_CONTACT_THRESHOLDS were tuned against these bogus near-zero reads — wrist_roll free-motion load peaks at 272 vs its 180 threshold, so a correctly-sampled load-watch would false-trigger on free motion. Thresholds MUST be re-tuned against real travel load profiles after the fix. WHY 604 TESTS MISSED IT: tests/test_gentle.py fake bus sets self._positions[motor] = position and bumps load inside write_goal_position — the servo teleports and load materializes on the commanding call, so the suite is structurally blind to early-sampling. FIX SHAPE: poll present_position + present_load DURING travel until measured arrival or timeout; contact = load>threshold while position is not advancing; terminate on measured arrival, not on commanded ticks exhausted; then re-tune thresholds. BLOCKS: all `arm explore` mapping runs are worthless until this lands.
